nameMeanings.name: Capella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jaime, Luis, Manuel, Angel, Cesar, Gerardo, Horacio, Javier, Mario, Miguel, Milagros.  Italian Primo, Ennio, Giovanni.
Catalan and Aragonese: topographic name from capella ‘chapel’ for someone who lived by a chapel, or a habitational name from a place called Capella in Aragon (Huesca).

variants.name: Cappella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Luigi, Ugo.
habitational name from any of numerous places called with cappella ‘chapel’, or a topographic name for someone who lived by a chapel.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: Joan Capella was born about 1297, in Edinburgh, Scotland. She married Sir John Preston in 1313. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1360, in Edinburgh, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan Capella est née environ 1297, à Edinburgh, Écosse. Elle a épousé Sir John Preston en 1313. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1360, à Edinburgh, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Joan Capella wurde um 1297 in Edinburgh, Scotland geboren. Sie hat 1313 Sir John Preston geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1360 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Edinburgh, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Joan Capella è nata il circa 1297 nel Edinburgh, Scotland. Lei sposato Sir John Preston nel 1313. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1360, nel Edinburgh, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Capellaは約1297年にスコットランドエディンブルグで生まれた。1313年にSir John Prestonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1360年にイギリススコットランドエディンブルグで64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Capella(은)는 대략 1297년 스컷랜드 에딘버그h에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1313년 Sir John Preston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1360년 영국 스코틀랜드 에딘버그h에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.
